# UC-22.56.17 · TSA-SD Public Transit + FTA-grantee Cyber Incident Reporting (NPRM-aligned, pre-Final-Rule operational scaffold)

> **Criticality:** High &middot; **Difficulty:** Intermediate &middot; **Pillar:** Security &middot; **Type:** Compliance, Security, Governance &middot; **Wave:** Walk &middot; **Status:** Verified

*Same 24-hour reporting rule as railroads — but for buses and city transit systems, once the final version of the rule is published.*

---

## Description

NPRM-aligned 24-hour reporting for public transit. Operates as a scaffold today; tightens automatically once the Final Rule is published.

## Value

Major transit agencies (NJT, MTA, BART, WMATA, Caltrain, MUNI, MetroLink) face FTA + state + TSA pressure; the NPRM-aligned scaffold avoids last-minute scramble on Final Rule.

## Implementation

Same as UC-22.56.9. Adjust schema once Final Rule clarifies covered-entity threshold.

## Evidence

Saved search `UC-22.56.17` archived every 30 minutes to `audit_evidence` with `tsa_sd_clause="TSA-SD-transit-NPRM-Nov2024-s2"`.

## Control test

### Positive scenario

Trigger synthetic IR with `tsa_transit_in_scope=true` and 23-hour-old awareness. UC-22.56.17 MUST escalate.

### Negative scenario

Submit within 24h. UC-22.56.17 MUST record `submission_status=on_time`.

## Detailed Implementation

## Prerequisites

Same as UC-22.56.10.

## Step 1 — Transit register

```ini
[tsa_transit_agency_lookup]
field.transit_agency_id = string
field.agency_name = string
field.tsa_in_scope = string
field.fta_grant_recipient = string
field.apta_cyber_standard = string
```

## Step 2 — SOAR playbook `tsa_transit_cisa_submission`

Mirrors `tsa_passenger_rail_cisa_submission` with FTA branch.

## Step 3 — Runbook

Same structure as UC-22.56.1; FTA branch on grantees.

## Step 4 — Troubleshooting

- **Final Rule lag**: monitor TSA Federal Register; adjust schema/scope on publication.

**TSA-specific failure modes**:
- **State-level transit cyber laws** (CA, NY): state regulators may impose additional clocks; coordinate with state CSO.

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:sn_si_incident" tsa_transit_in_scope=true tsa_reportable_category=*
| eval awareness_time = coalesce(tsa_awareness_time, opened_at)
| eval cisa_submission_time = strptime(cisa_submission_time, "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S")
| eval hours_since_awareness = round((now() - awareness_time) / 3600, 2)
| eval hours_to_submission = case(
    isnotnull(cisa_submission_time), round((cisa_submission_time - awareness_time) / 3600, 2),
    1=1, hours_since_awareness)
| eval submission_status = case(
    isnotnull(cisa_submission_time) AND hours_to_submission <= 24, "on_time",
    isnotnull(cisa_submission_time) AND hours_to_submission > 24, "late",
    isnull(cisa_submission_time) AND hours_since_awareness > 23 AND hours_since_awareness <= 24, "clock_burning",
    isnull(cisa_submission_time) AND hours_since_awareness > 24, "missed",
    1=1, "in_window")
| where submission_status IN ("clock_burning","missed","late")
| table incident_id transit_agency_id tsa_reportable_category awareness_time cisa_submission_time hours_to_submission submission_status
```

## Visualization

(Panel 1) Clock-burn. (Panel 2) Submission performance. (Panel 3) Per-agency status. (Panel 4) NPRM-vs-Final-Rule scope tracking.

## Known False Positives

1. **APTA-aligned existing programs**: many transit agencies already operate APTA-aligned cyber programs; align UC scope to NPRM-defined covered transit.

**Suppression mechanism**: KV Store `tsa_22_56_17_exceptions`.

## References

- [TSA Surface NPRM (89 FR 88488)](https://www.federalregister.gov/documents/2024/11/07/2024-25712/enhancing-surface-cyber-risk-management)
- [FTA Cybersecurity Information](https://www.transit.dot.gov/regulations-and-guidance/safety/cybersecurity)
- [APTA Cybersecurity Standards (PT-CCS-RP-001)](https://www.apta.com/research-technical-resources/standards/)
